name: Tine Piano
explanation: A polyphonic electric tine-piano instrument. Each voice is a struck metal tine modelled with two-operator FM — a sine carrier at pitch plus a higher-ratio modulator whose index decays fast for the bright bell/bark attack that melts into a mellow sine body. Velocity drives the bark amount and brightness, a natural two-stage decay keeps notes singing, and a gentle opposed-phase stereo tremolo adds the classic suitcase sway. Controls: Bell (attack FM amount), Decay, Tone, Tremolo Depth, Tremolo Rate and Level.
